issues: asymmetry: central spider figure appendages show slight asymmetry in leg placement and length across diagonal axes
outline_inconsistency: octagonal border ring has minor 1px/2px thickness variation at corner transitions
semantic_ambiguity: central figure reads as both spider and abstract attack symbol — intent unclear at 4x, legs vs. swords vs. decorative spokes not definitively resolved at 8x
pixel_jitter: diagonal X-cross pattern behind central body shows single stray pixels at two intersection points deviating from expected 45-degree Bresenham lines
reasoning: Pixel art style effectively masks most classic AI tells — no AA edges, no gradient shading, no noise in solid fills, no color bleed. Hard tones are proper and palette is appropriately limited. However, adversarial inspection at 8x reveals four marginal concerns: (1) the spider figure's eight appendages are not symmetric across both axes — top-left and bottom-right pairs are ~1px shorter than the other pair; (2) the octagonal ring border fluctuates between 1px and 2px at the NW and SE corner transitions rather than maintaining uniform 1px; (3) the central icon semantic is ambiguous — a competent human pixel artist would commit clearly to spider vs. crossed-swords motif; the hybrid reads as under-resolved concept which is a common AI conflation; (4) two stray pixels appear off the expected diagonal line in the X-cross background pattern. None of these individually constitute a hard fail, but the combination of minor asymmetry plus semantic conflation plus stray pixels is consistent with AI generation of pixel-art-style assets rather than hand-crafted work. No sub-pixel features, no melting, no gradient shading observed. Overall: marginal — passes stylistic consistency but fails on symmetry discipline and semantic clarity.
